page: OVHcloud c3-4 is a compute-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor operating at 2.3 GHz, configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not feature any GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test placing in the top 10% at 3083.56 and the Geekbench single-core score ranking in the top 25% at 1920.0. However, multi-core and memory performance show limitations. The Geekbench multi-core score of 3460.0 and Passmark CPU score of 5442.44 fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak cached read-only speed of 294.72 GB/sec, though uncached large block read at 61.56 GB/sec and write at 29.93 GB/sec bandwidth remain average. Static web serving throughput is poor, registering at 2.57 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-4 offers dedicated CPU allocation on modern AMD Milan hardware, making it suitable for workloads requiring consistent single-threaded execution without resource sharing. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-CPU ratio and weak multi-threaded scaling, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded compilation. Appropriate workloads include lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small-scale LLM inference tasks using small models.
description: OVHcloud c3-4 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) running at 2.3 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core scores in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting a clear tradeoff between single-threaded efficiency and multi-threaded scaling. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution without high memory demands. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-4 is a compute-optimized KVM instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance with limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-4 is a compute-optimized KVM instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance delivering strong single-threaded performance and local NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores (2 vCPUs) at 2.3 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests
KVM hypervisor virtualization with dedicated CPU allocation
Best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and light inference
